ksa_text: Skill in building and maintaining data service catalogs that document available data products, access methods, service-level expectations, data ownership, support contacts, and usage guidelines for consumers.

nice.top_element_label: Knowledge of digital evidence cataloging tools and techniques

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Included: skill maps directly to AI Governance Manager scope — GOV-specific signals detected ("data ownership", "guideline"). STRM composite is moderate-to-strong (0.4154). Domain Operations & Program Management (affinity 0.65) is a natural fit for this governance role. Seniority is appropriate for manager-level level.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: External check: no matches in governance job postings or certification curricula (CDMP, DAMA, IAPP CIPP/CIPM, NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001). Would a working AI Governance Manager self-report this as a required competency?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Challenge answered: the governance competency is implicit in AI Governance Manager practice (domain affinity 0.65, 2 GOV signal(s)). Framework vocabulary matching underrepresents many legitimate governance competencies.
